stories.headline: Iran Says Hormuz Strait Now Completely Open for Commercial Ships
stories.summary:
Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for commercial traffic, a major step toward ending a war with the US and Israel that’s sent energy prices surging.
“In line with the ceasefire in Lebanon, the passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open for the remaining period of ceasefire,” Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on X. Ships can move on the “coordinated route as already an

stories.headline: Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is Completely Open for Commercial Ships
stories.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is completely open for commercial traffic, a major step toward ending the war with the US and Israel. Oil prices plunged on the news. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran foreign minister says Strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’
stories.summary: Iran says 'passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz' open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.headline: Israeli attacks kill several over two days in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
stories.summary: As Gaza violence continues, activist says Israeli settlers torch vehicles, attack homes in occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Oil tumbles after Iran pledges to open strait during Lebanon truce
stories.summary: Crude falls 10 per cent following statement from Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi

stories.headline: BBC in Iran: 'Tehran does not think it has lost this war'
stories.summary: Lyse Doucet says Iranians want a solution to the long-running animosity with the US, but leaders is not willing to make a deal on Washington's terms.

stories.headline: Dollar Wipes Out Iran War Gain as Tehran Says Hormuz Is Open
stories.summary: The dollar wiped out all the gains it registered since the US-Iran war began after Tehran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for commercial traffic, further denting demand for haven assets.

stories.headline: ‘Waiting without answers’: Gaza mother’s fear for her three imprisoned sons
stories.summary: As Palestinians mark Prisoners' Day, Inaam al-Dahdouh waits for news of her three sons, taken by Israel two years ago.

stories.headline: Australia's most decorated living soldier released on bail over war crime charges
stories.summary: Lawyers argued that Ben Roberts-Smith wouldn't be able to defend himself properly from prison.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi shooting survivors
stories.summary: On the fourth day of their Australian visit, the royal couple pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi shooting.

stories.headline: Wall Street Turns Gloomy on the Dollar as Haven Demand Fades
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank AG and Wells Fargo & Co. are among banks declaring the dollar’s war-driven haven rally is likely over as the ceasefire between the US and Iran prompts investors to seek riskier assets.

stories.headline: Oil and Gas Prices Plunge After Iran Says Hormuz Strait Is Open
stories.summary: Oil and gas prices plunged early Friday after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is now completely open to traffic. But Bloomberg's Mia Gindis says it may take some time for tanker traffic to bounce back. She reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Trump officials say the program is vital to national security, but skeptics — including some Republicans — have stonewalled its reauthorization without changes to protect civil liberties.

stories.headline: Empty LNG Tankers Attempt Hormuz Crossing Into Persian Gulf

More than 3,000 Hollywood actors, directors and screenwriters have penned a letter opposing the $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery by Paramount Skydance Corp., citing concerns about job losses and higher costs with fewer choices for consumers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

Gold Prices Surge After Iran Says Hormuz Strait Completely Open
stories.summary: Gold jumped after Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for commercial traffic, a major step toward ending a war with the US and Israel that has sent energy prices soaring and threatened economic growth.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran agrees to hand over ‘nuclear dust’

stories.headline: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
